item.title: Korean Firms Turn to Short-Term Funding as Bond Yields Climb
item.description: South Korean firms have increasingly turned to short-term financing as rising government bond yields drive up borrowing costs in the corporate debt market.

item.title: South Africa’s Kganyago Vows to Get Inflation Rate Back to 3%
item.description: South African central bank Governor Lesetja Kganyago promised that officials will lower the inflation rate back to its 3% target despite headwinds from the Iran war.

item.title: Euro-Zone Inflation Surpasses 3% for First Time Since 2023
item.description: Euro-area inflation topped 3% for the first time in more than 2 1/2 years, cementing expectations for an interest-rate hike when the European Central Bank meets next week.

item.title: Galaxy Digital Sets Up Prediction Market OTC Derivatives Desk
item.description: Crypto finance conglomerate Galaxy Digital Inc. has launched a trading desk to offer large investors better access to prediction markets via over-the-counter derivatives similar to those used in markets from interest rates to commodities.

item.title: Fed Warned on Shrinking Balance Sheet in Lookback at Powell Era
item.description: The Federal Reserve should avoid any move to significantly shrink its balance sheet, two prominent economists warned Tuesday — arguing against a strategy that the US central bank’s new chairman has specifically called for.

item.title: Russia Says It Found Foreign Spyware on Top Officials’ Phones
item.description: Russia’s Federal Security Service said it uncovered a large-scale operation by foreign intelligence agencies to implant spyware on senior officials’ mobile phones.

item.title: Swiss Watch Exports Drop, Skewed by 2025 Rush Before US Tariffs
item.description: Swiss watch exports fell in April from a year earlier, when companies were rushing to get stock into the US ahead of higher tariffs.

item.title: UBS Offers First AT1 Bond Since Swiss Authorities Paused Reforms
item.description: UBS Group AG is set to raise Additional Tier 1 capital in US dollars, its first foray into the market since Switzerland paused plans to tighten rules on the riskiest type of bank debt.

item.title: UK Mortgage Approvals Hit 15-Month High Despite Iran War
item.description: UK mortgage approvals unexpectedly rose in April as the housing market displayed ongoing resilience to the economic fallout from war in Iran.

item.title: MediaTek Pledges Hiring Spree to Boost Push Into New AI Spheres
item.description: Taiwanese chipmaker MediaTek Inc. will hike hiring to support a push into new AI activities, joining fellow tech firms in assuaging concerns about job losses in the era of artificial intelligence.

item.title: South Africa Business Mood Slips as Iran War Upends Rate Outlook
item.description: South African business sentiment weakened in the second quarter as consumers faced fresh pressures from the Iran war that’s clouded the outlook for inflation and interest rates.

item.title: Incoming Tepco Chair Open to Alliance Partners Outside of Japan
item.description: The incoming chairman of Tokyo Electric Power Co. said he is open to forming alliances with companies outside of Japan as the nation’s largest utility seeks to restructure its operations and fund the massive cost of cleaning up the Fukushima nuclear disaster.

item.title: India’s Key Monsoon Rains Set to Arrive Days After Typical Start
item.description: India’s annual monsoon rains that are crucial for farm output are likely to arrive around Thursday at Kerala state on the country’s southern tip, a few days after the official start date.

item.title: Euro’s Global Role Gains Slightly But Still Distant From Dollar
item.description: International use of the euro rose slightly in 2025 but stayed well below that of the dollar, highlighting the difficulty of mounting a global challenge to the greenback’s supremacy.

item.title: India Bargains Hard in Key US, UK Trade Talks This Week
item.description: India is seeking to safeguard exporters’ interests in trade negotiations with the US and UK this week, with implications for trade deals with two of its major partners.

item.title: Bank of Thailand Governor Sees No Need to Raise Rates ‘For Now’
item.description: Bank of Thailand Governor Vitai Ratanakorn said the current policy rate remains “appropriate” even as inflation accelerates and some central bank peers turn more hawkish in response to higher energy prices.

item.title: EU Lawmakers Clear US Trade Deal for Final Approval
item.description: European Union lawmakers gave preliminary approval to a US trade deal, clearing a path for final ratification before President Donald Trump’s threatened deadline to impose new tariffs.

item.title: India to Unveil Broader Gauge for Inflation in Data Revamp
item.description: India is set to launch a Producer Price Index for the first time, a step toward aligning with most advanced economies, giving policymakers a broader measure of inflation that captures price pressures across both goods and services in the world’s fastest-growing major economy.

item.title: Philip Morris Cuts Outlook on $500 Million Canada Writedown
item.description: Philip Morris International Inc. cut its profit forecast for this fiscal year after writing down the value of its investment in its Canadian affiliate by $500 million.

item.title: Goldman Said to Work on Retailer IPO in Boost to Its Kuwait Push
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. is working on a potential initial public offering of a Kuwaiti online beauty and fashion retailer, a deal that could mark the Wall Street bank’s first listing mandate in the Gulf nation as it seeks to expand there.

item.title: Cambodia Seeks Rare UN Ruling in $300 Billion Thai Energy Spat
item.description: Cambodia has initiated rare United Nations compulsory conciliation proceedings against Thailand to resolve a decades-long maritime border dispute, after talks collapsed over an area both countries say holds $300 billion worth of oil and gas.

item.title: SK Hynix to Double Wafer Capacity to Ease Memory Chip Crunch
item.description: SK Hynix Inc. plans to double its memory chip wafer capacity over the coming half-decade, a major expansion that should help ease a global shortage of an essential component of AI.

item.title: Arm May Hit $15 Billion AI Chip Revenue Goal Early, CEO Says
item.description: Arm Holdings Plc may achieve its target of $15 billion in sales of its own chips earlier than anticipated, its chief executive officer said, reflecting stronger-than-projected demand from the AI boom.

item.title: STMicro Nearly Doubles Outlook for 2026 Data Center Revenue
item.description: STMicroelectronics NV nearly doubled its forecast for revenue from its data centers business to $1 billion this year, fueled by booming demand for artificial intelligence infrastructure.

item.title: China’s Lab-Grown Diamonds Emerge as Unlikely Winner in AI Boom
item.description: China’s lab-grown diamonds are emerging as a surprising beneficiary of the artificial intelligence boom, with demand climbing while they gain traction as a key component in advanced chipmaking.

item.title: Commodities in ‘Super-Squeeze’ as Hormuz Risks Build, HSBC Says
item.description: Commodities are in a “super-squeeze” that will worsen if the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively shut, according to HSBC Holdings Plc.

item.title: RBI May Have Sold Gold to Save FX Reserves, BE Analysis Shows
item.description: India’s central bank may have offloaded a portion of its gold holdings to shield its foreign-currency assets from the cascading fallout of the war in the Middle East, according to an analysis by Bloomberg Economics based on publicly available data.

item.title: The Iran War Is Pushing the Global Gas Trade Into the Shadows
item.description: Running out of time and patience, major producers like Qatar are ripping up the maritime rulebook — with potentially lasting consequences.

item.title: Morgan Stanley Drawn Into Probe Over €5.7 Billion Bolloré Sale
item.description: Morgan Stanley risks being embroiled in a French criminal case by a group of communities in Africa that suspect the US bank helped Vincent Bolloré launder money stemming from the disposal of an allegedly corrupt €5.7 billion ($6.6 billion) asset.

item.title: Global Funds Pull Out a Decade’s Worth of India Equity Inflows
item.description: Overseas investors’ cumulative net equity investments in India have slid to a near-decade low after relentless selling, highlighting the fading attractiveness of the nation’s $4.9 trillion stock market.
